title: The words the happy say by Emily Dickinson — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
The words the happy say
Are paltry melody
But those the silent feel
Are beautiful --
meaning: "The words the happy say" by Emily Dickinson is a deep poem consisting of 4 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "The words the happy say Are paltry melody...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Emily Dickinson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
